# Luis H. Reyes (Project of Unit Operations)

<summary>자막: Luis H. Reyes (Project of Unit Operations)</summary>

Hello, I'm Luis Reyes, an associate professor in the Department of Chemical and Food Engineering at the University of Aand. In my period of unit operation course, I'm tasked with preparing the next generation of chemical engineers for a rapidly evolving industry. The rise of generative AI presented a powerful opportunity, but also a critical dilemma. How would do we leverage these incredible tools without undermining the very development of critical thinking that we extract to foster? How do we ensure AI enhance it rather than replaces student learning? How do we design assignment that work with AI rather than against IT? Frameworks like the Artificial Intelligence Assessment Scale created by Perkins and collaborators are an excellent star. They provide a useful contract between educators and students on the acceptable levels of AI use in an assignment. In fact, our own university official guidelines recommend that professor use this scale to define the authorized level of AI involvement for each academic activity. But here's the challenge. While the framework is great for setting the rules, it doesn't guide the professor on how to design pedagogically sound activities for that specific rule set. It presumes the educator already knows the best way to craft a task that benefit the students at use ai. And more importantly, it doesn't account for the most critical variable the student cognitive development stage. This is the precise gap that my colleagues and I engineer a solution for the cognitive AI Synergy Framework or cas. It builds on a simple premise. Educators know how to teach and know their craft, but not necessarily know ai. So let's ground AI integration on what we know best. How a student learn CS maps the level of AI integration directly to establish cognitive development stages. For instance, at the propositional level, students are just learning to apply knowledge to solve structural problems. At this stage, AI is excellent for verifying their problem solving approach, but not forgiving them the solution outright. However, at the highest metacognitive level, students are reflecting on their own learning processes. At this expanded stage, they can use AI as a full collaborative partner, helping them to plan and evaluate and even enhance their own learning strategies. To make this framework practical and scalable, we build the CAS implementation assistant. It is an AI power tool that guides professor. Through designing educational activities, a professor can input their course details, the student cognitive level, and the learning objective. The assistant then provides tailored activity plans from examples and ethical guidelines all based on the CAST framework. The impact of this is more, is a more authentic and effective learning experience. It lowers the barriers for faculty to use AI with confidence. Our workshop shows that professor find it highly effective for redesigning their courses with an overall satisfaction rating of 4.5 or five. Let me show you a tangible example of how this works In my own class, the cas assistant guides me through a series of questions to design an activity tailored to the course requirement, my student's cognitive level, and the best practice for implementation. It can even help me update another activity that doesn't use generative ai, transforming it into one that does. By the end, I have a rate to use package and state by state guide worksheets and even a student learning cards that I can bring straight into the classroom. Now in my class, a key challenge for a student is simply learning how to start a research project. Instead of instead of just telling them to do a literature review, I use CAST to create an activity to guide them based on my course goals and the students' cognitive level cas pro I make with a complete activity guide to a structured entire process. I made it pretty, as you can see, it's not just about the tool, it's about following a pedagogically sound sequence. An example of challenge could be developing unique sourdough started from Colombian ingredients, as you can see on the screen. And here's the output. These questions are fantastic. They start with the fundamentals, like what is the function of a sourdough started and progress to advanced topics like what are the challenge for commercial application and what are the future research trends? When AI is integrated thoughtfully guide by a pedagogical framework like cas. It doesn't just make a student faster. It makes their world more rigorous, thorough, and professional. The cognitive AI synergy framework provides a roadmap for integrated AI into education responsibly and effectively achieve the focus from a technology driven approach to a student-centered one. This ensure that as AI continues to evolve our pedagogical principles, our deep understanding of how students learn and develop remains the guide. By doing so, we can empower both educators and students to truly thrive in this new era. Thank you.
